steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a boundaries question. The sentence states that the manuscripts “have survived from roughly the sixteenth century to the present ______ being passed down through private libraries.” The phrase “being passed down” is a gerund phrase explaining the method of survival. It must attach to the main clause with the preposition “by” — no punctuation may interrupt that prepositional link.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘day by’: Completes “present day” as the endpoint, then introduces “by being passed down” as a prepositional phrase with no intervening punctuation — grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘day; by’: A semicolon must separate two independent clauses. “By being passed down through private libraries” is a prepositional phrase, not an independent clause, so the semicolon is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘day. By’: A period creates a sentence fragment: “By being passed down through private libraries in the city of Timbuktu, Mali” would stand alone with no subject or finite verb. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘day by,’: A comma after “by” illegally separates the preposition from its object (“being passed down”), breaking the prepositional phrase. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
